{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question is whether SMAD6 variants can cause craniosynostosis. Multiple documents from MEDCORP support this connection. Document [2] states that in sagittal craniosynostosis, the most common mutation is in the SMAD6 gene. Document [3] notes that mutations in SMAD6, TCF12, ERF, and MSX2 have been associated with non-syndromic cases of craniosynostosis. Document [5] specifically mentions that the most notable genetic mutations in non-syndromic metopic synostosis are FREM1 and SMAD6. Document [10] confirms that ERF and SMAD6 gene mutations are associated with metopic craniosynostosis. These references consistently link SMAD6 mutations to non-syndromic craniosynostosis. Therefore, the evidence strongly supports that SMAD6 variants can cause craniosynostosis.", "answer_choice": "A"}
